pr.title: Mark ft_0CD1 as matching, run ninja apply, fix splits.txt
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Marked melee/ft/ft_0CD1.c as matching in configure.py, corrected two ftdevice.c OSReport string literals, and synchronized generated GALE01 config outputs for symbols and splits. The split fixes rename stale item object paths from it_2F2B/it_27CF to descriptive Yoshi item files, while the symbol update changes an sdata2 float label from ftCo_804D8EF8 to @183.

key_files.role: Changed ft_0CD1 from Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching).
Corrected two OSReport messages from generic coll wording to wind and catch wording.
Updated split object paths for Yoshi-related item files: ityoshitongue.c and ityoshiegglay.c.
Updated local sdata2 float symbol label ftCo_804D8EF8 to @183.

what_changed: In configure.py, melee/ft/ft_0CD1.c was moved from NonMatching to Matching inside the MatchingFor object list.
In ftdevice.c, ftCo_800C06E8 now reports "fighter chk device wind func num over!" instead of "fighter chk device coll func num over!" before asserting.
In ftdevice.c, ftCo_800C0764 now reports "fighter chk device catch func num over!" instead of "fighter chk device coll func num over!" before asserting.
In config/GALE01/splits.txt, melee/it/items/it_2F2B.c was renamed to melee/it/items/ityoshitongue.c for the 0x802F2BFC-0x802F2D98 split.
In config/GALE01/splits.txt, melee/it/items/it_27CF.c was renamed to melee/it/items/ityoshiegglay.c for the 0x802F2F34-0x802F3394 split.
In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, the .sdata2:0x804D8EF8 local float label changed from ftCo_804D8EF8 to @183, consistent with generated symbol output.
smart_moves: The PR pairs the matching-status flip with the small source string changes needed to make ft_0CD1 match, rather than only changing build metadata.
Running generated config updates after marking the file matching kept configure.py, symbols.txt, and splits.txt in sync.
The ftdevice.c edits are minimal and high-confidence: only string literals changed, preserving function structure while correcting binary data contents.
The splits.txt fix removes stale raw item module names and points the linker/split metadata at already-known descriptive item source names.
decomp_lessons: Exact diagnostic strings matter for matching; even assert-adjacent OSReport text must match the original binary byte-for-byte.

When a source file becomes matching, update configure.py from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...) and run the repository's apply/regeneration step so generated config files reflect the new state.
Do not keep a semantic-looking symbol name unless it is supported by the generated symbol state; anonymous/local floats may need @NNN labels such as @183.
splits.txt should track current source file names, not obsolete placeholder names, especially after item files are renamed from raw identifiers to descriptive module names.
This slice does not show the full ft_0CD1 matching work, only the final metadata flip and small data/string corrections.
naming_conventions: Item files in splits.txt use descriptive lowercase names when known, e.g. melee/it/items/ityoshitongue.c instead of it_2F2B.c and melee/it/items/ityoshiegglay.c instead of it_27CF.c.
Unidentified common fighter functions still use ftCo_800xxxxx address-style names, as seen with ftCo_800C06E8 and ftCo_800C0764.
Generated local sdata2 labels may use @NNN syntax for unnamed constants, e.g. @183 at .sdata2:0x804D8EF8.
The matching build list uses Object(Matching, "path") and Object(NonMatching, "path") entries in configure.py.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Correct string literals to match original rodata/sdata contents without changing control flow.
Use the Matching/NonMatching object list in configure.py as the authoritative matching-status switch.
After changing matching status, run the apply/regeneration workflow so symbols.txt and splits.txt reflect the updated build outputs.
No inline assembly tactics, register-allocation tricks, or reviewer-provided asm analysis are present in this PR slice.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided slice.
No explicit PR body or human discussion was available; the inferred intent comes from the title and diff.
The PR was merged the same day, suggesting the change was treated as a small matching/config maintenance update, but the slice does not include reviewer rationale.
